page: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by custom 4th Generation Intel Xeon Scalable processors (model 8488C). The system features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz and a 105 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The bare-metal architecture provides direct access to the underlying hardware without hypervisor overhead, supporting advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in memory-intensive and multi-threaded workloads. The server achieved top-tier status in Geekbench multi-core testing (21,623.0) and Passmark CPU evaluation (88,827.32). Memory bandwidth is a key strength, with cached small-block read-only bandwidth reaching 7,016.18 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 229.99 GB/sec. Additionally, the platform demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, processing 104.51 million Redis SET operations per second, and shows top-tier performance in static web serving throughput at 85.45 GB/sec.

While the r7i.metal-24xl offers high memory capacity and memory bandwidth, it does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage-heavy or graphics-accelerated workloads must rely on network-attached resources or alternative instance types. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal configuration maximizes resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for large-scale enterprise applications. This server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including large in-memory databases like Redis, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, delivering high memory bandwidth, as evidenced by a cached read-only bandwidth of 7,016.18 GB/sec. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on a 38 Gbps network baseline. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and static web serving. The bare-metal architecture eliminates hypervisor overhead, offering high resource density and direct hardware access. This profile makes the r7i.metal-24xl highly efficient for demanding enterprise workloads, such as large in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, optimized for in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR5 RAM, built for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding in-memory databases and enterprise applications.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8488C processors at 3.2 GHz
768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 5 GHz clock speed
Bare-metal architecture with direct hardware access and no hypervisor overhead
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
Best suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and web serving
